headline: {{city}} DUCTED A/C WITH ZONING
prompt_text:
You are being given five attached images. Use ONLY Image 1 and Image 2 in your output — Images 3, 4 and 5 are unused for this run (this client's brands are ActronAir, Hisense and Panasonic, not Daikin or Mitsubishi, and only one neutral condenser is needed).

IMAGE 1 (reference ad for A8): Use as a STRUCTURAL TEMPLATE only. Mirror its composition skeleton — city-prefixed headline anchored at the top, hero on the left, oversized price treatment on the right, urgency badge in a corner, brand identifiers along an edge. DO NOT copy its sky-blue background palette, its 3D house diagram, its "$9,990" fixed-price treatment, its "50% OFF" badge, or its brand list. The reference contains a misspelling ("0% INTREST") and a prohibited "0% interest" claim — both forbidden in your output. Generate a fresh palette, fresh hero, fresh price treatment, and fresh badges per the brief below.

IMAGE 2 (locked condenser cond_neutral_1 — unbranded double-fan unit, 3/4 angle, neutral white casing): Use this image AS-IS as the hero. Place it on the left half of the canvas, roughly 40% canvas width, vertically centred between the headline and the bottom edge. Do NOT modify the unit's silhouette, fan count, vent grilles, side panel, branding (it is unbranded — keep it that way; do not add a logo to it), or proportions. You may apply a subtle accent glow or a soft gradient backdrop behind it for palette integration, but the unit itself must remain pixel-faithful to the source.

Create a square 1080×1080 social media ad for Jonathan Air, a residential ducted air conditioning installer servicing Queensland, Australia.

FUNNEL CONTEXT
This is a Warm/Hot, city-anchored sale ad — the volume-driver. The audience already knows what ducted A/C is. The job is to push them past the line with an unmissable local offer, real urgency, and a per-week price that makes the decision feel small. No explainer work needed.

CRITICAL CLARITY RULE
Within ONE SECOND of seeing this ad, a scroller must understand it is for DUCTED AIR CONDITIONING in QUEENSLAND on a limited-time sale. Both "DUCTED A/C" and "QUEENSLAND" must sit prominently in the headline at thumbnail-readable size. Premium aesthetic does not override this. Clarity beats cleverness.

MOOD & STYLE
Aggressive direct-response. The picked variables ("Final Days, Final Stock", "LIMITED TIME") demand urgency, not clearance-clean polish. Pick a fresh dark high-contrast palette per generation — explicitly NOT the reference ad's sky-blue. Suggested territory (pick one or invent in this register): deep navy with hot orange or electric coral accent; near-black charcoal with electric yellow accent; oxblood/maroon with cream accent; midnight green with bright amber accent. Choose fresh aggressive bold display typography for the headline (modern condensed sans-serif works well — think volume-retail) and a clean uppercase sans for body. Slightly loud, slightly urgent, but professional retail — not a polished brochure.

LAYOUT (top to bottom)

1. TOP HEADLINE BLOCK — top quarter, full canvas width
Two-line stacked oversized bold uppercase headline, 60px clear of the top edge, sized to dominate the upper third:
   - Line 1: "QUEENSLAND" — set in the contrasting accent colour. The city anchor, immediately visible.
   - Line 2: "DUCTED A/C WITH ZONING" — set in white or cream, slightly larger or equal weight to Line 1. The product clarity element.
The two lines must read as one sentence and BOTH must be unmissable at thumbnail size. No tracking-out, no italic — bold, planted, urgent.

2. HERO CONDENSER — left half, mid-canvas, ~40% canvas width
The unbranded double-fan condenser (Image 2) sits on the left, vertically centred between the headline and the brand stack. Add a soft accent-coloured glow or gradient halo behind it so the white casing reads cleanly against the dark background — do not tint the unit itself heavily, do not modify shape, fans, or panels. Photo-real, dimensional, hero-treatment.

3. PRICE BLOCK — right half, vertically aligned with the condenser's centre, left-aligned text
Stacked, with hierarchy:
   - Top: small uppercase caps in cream/white: "FROM"
   - Centre dominant numeral: "$78" — oversized, the largest typographic element in the ad after the headline, set in white or cream so it pops against the dark background
   - Beside or just below the numeral: medium uppercase suffix in the accent colour: "/WEEK"
   - Beneath the price: small uppercase caps, two short lines: "FULLY INSTALLED" / "INTEREST-FREE FINANCE AVAILABLE"

4. VALUE STACK — directly beneath the price block, right half
Three short tick-bullets, accent-colour ticks, white text, evenly spaced, each line clearly readable at thumbnail size:
   ✓ Final Days, Final Stock
   ✓ $1,299 in Free Bonuses Included
   ✓ Locally Installed by Queensland Experts

5. CTA BUTTON — bottom-left zone, beneath the condenser, inside the 60px safe zone
Rounded pill button in the accent colour with bold uppercase dark or contrasting text:
"QUEENSLAND HOMEOWNERS — CLAIM TODAY"
Sized large enough to read instantly at thumbnail. If "QUEENSLAND HOMEOWNERS" stretches the pill too wide, allow the line to break inside the pill across two tight lines — do not shrink the type below thumbnail-readable size to fit a single line.

6. URGENCY BADGE — top-right corner
Circular burst badge, slightly tilted (5–10 degrees), accent-colour fill, bold uppercase dark or white text inside on three lines:
   - Top arc: "LIMITED TIME"
   - Centre dominant: "25%"
   - Bottom: "OFF"
Sized to be unmissable at thumbnail — meaningful, not decorative. Sits within the 60px safe zone from top and right edges.

7. BRAND STACK — right edge, vertical column (NOT a horizontal bottom strip)
Three brand wordmarks/logos stacked vertically along the right edge of the canvas, sitting between the price block area and the bottom of the canvas, evenly spaced. Render each in its native brand colours and correct typography:
   - ActronAir (red/black wordmark)
   - Hisense (red wordmark)
   - Panasonic (blue wordmark)
This vertical brand stack is A8's structural signature. Do NOT compose them as a horizontal strip across the bottom (that is A7's territory and is forbidden here). Do NOT include Daikin, Mitsubishi Electric, Hitachi, or any brand visible in the reference ad — those are not this client's brands.

8. AUSTRALIAN-OWNED TRUST BADGE — bottom-right corner accent
A small circular seal in navy/cream/gold visual language: navy fill, cream rim, gold accent, with "AUSTRALIAN OWNED" set around the rim and a small Australia silhouette icon centred. Generate fresh in the ad's palette context — do not embed a foreign-coloured badge that fights the rest of the ad. Sized as a meaningful but secondary trust element, sitting beside or just below the brand stack. Per HR19, this badge is included because client.australian_owned = true. Do NOT include a "Family Owned" badge (client.family_owned = false).

9. GOOGLE REVIEWS PILL — small horizontal pill, sits along the bottom edge inside the safe zone
Five gold/yellow stars + "113+ 5-STAR GOOGLE REVIEWS" in clean white text on a thin dark or accent-coloured pill. Small but legible at thumbnail — every digit and word readable. Earned by client.google_review_count clearing the HR08 ≥30 threshold.

GLOBAL HARD RULES
- 1080×1080 square aspect ratio, nothing else
- Maintain ~60px safe-zone padding inside all four edges — no headlines, badges, CTAs, price text, or value stack inside this margin. Brand stack may sit close to but not touching the right edge.
- Every text element readable at thumbnail size on a phone — both headline lines, the price numeral, the "/WEEK" suffix, the "FULLY INSTALLED · INTEREST-FREE FINANCE" caption, every value-stack tick, the CTA, the "25% OFF" badge, the "AUSTRALIAN OWNED" rim text, and the "113+ 5-STAR GOOGLE REVIEWS" pill must all be clearly legible
- Use the condenser (Image 2) AS PROVIDED — same shape, same fan configuration, same proportions, same unbranded casing. Soft palette glow/backdrop only. No structural changes, no added branding, no recolour of the casing.
- Brand list is exactly: ActronAir, Hisense, Panasonic — three logos maximum (HR05). Render in native brand colours.
- Do NOT include "0% interest" anywhere — the reference ad's "0% INTREST" is both a typo AND a prohibited finance claim. Use only "INTEREST-FREE FINANCE AVAILABLE".
- Do NOT include a fixed-price installation amount. Do NOT include a struck-through anchor price. This is the per-week variant.
- Do NOT add people, faces, hands, or any AI-generated portraits anywhere in the ad (HR04).
- Do NOT generate any house diagram, technical illustration, or zoning diagram from scratch (HR-DIAGRAMS). The reference ad's house diagram is NOT supplied as a locked component this run, so the condenser is the sole hero.
- Discount percentage on the urgency badge MUST be 25% (equals client.current_promo_pct).
- The brand stack MUST be vertical along the right edge — NOT a horizontal bottom strip.
- The headline MUST contain both "QUEENSLAND" and "DUCTED A/C", both unmissable at thumbnail.
- Australian English spellings throughout. "$" for AUD.
- Crop, mask, or paint out any stock-agency watermarks if any embedded asset carries them (HR18).
- Pick a fresh palette and fresh font pairing for THIS generation — do not default to navy+yellow if a sibling ad already used it. The only locked palette constraint is "dark high-contrast aggressive direct-response" and "explicitly NOT sky-blue".
- The overall feel must be aggressive direct-response and urgent. If the output reads as calm, polished, brochure-like, or clearance-clean, it has failed this brief.

headline: {{city}} DUCTED HEATING SALE
prompt_text:
You are being given five attached images:

IMAGE 1 (reference ad — A8 structural template): Use this as a strong layout, density, and energy reference. Match its full-canvas dominant headline, oversized price block sitting opposite a hero condenser/illustration, multi-brand side stack on the right, and bright winter-coded mood. DO NOT copy its exact palette, illustration, or specific text. Generate a fresh palette and font pairing per generation. NOTE: the reference contains the misspelling "INTREST" and the phrase "0% INTREST" — DO NOT replicate either. Per HR07, "0% interest" in any form is forbidden — only "$0 UPFRONT" and "Interest-Free Finance Available" are acceptable affordability phrases.

IMAGE 2 (locked component — cond_neutral_1, neutral double-fan condenser at 3/4 angle): Use this image AS-IS as the hero condenser. Place on the right side of the canvas at roughly 35–40% canvas width, vertically anchored around the mid-canvas line. Do not modify its silhouette, fan count (two stacked fans), proportions, vents, panels, or surface. You may sit it on a soft circular glow or subtle radial backdrop in a slightly lighter palette tone for separation, but the unit itself remains identical to the source. If any stock watermark is visible (HR18), crop or paint it out — do not embed it.

IMAGES 3, 4, 5 (alternate condenser variants — cond_neutral_2, cond_daikin, cond_mitsubishi): DO NOT use. Client's brands are ActronAir, Hisense and Panasonic — neither Daikin nor Mitsubishi applies, and neutral_1 (IMAGE 2) is the chosen hero. Ignore IMAGES 3–5 entirely.

Create a square 1080×1080 social media ad for Jonathan Air, a residential ducted air conditioning installer servicing Queensland, Australia.

FUNNEL CONTEXT
Warm/Hot audience. This is the volume-driver state-anchored sale ad. Buyers already know what ducted A/C is — the job is to push them past the line with a bold geographic hook ("QUEENSLAND"), an unmissable per-week price, and a winter-heating angle that feels timely.

CRITICAL CLARITY RULE
A scroller must understand within ONE SECOND that this ad is for DUCTED HEATING & COOLING (whole-home ducted air conditioning) on sale across Queensland. The product category MUST appear unmissably. The operator-picked headline is "QUEENSLAND DUCTED HEATING SALE" — that runs as-is, but the sub-headline MUST contain the full phrase "DUCTED HEATING & COOLING" so the category is locked beyond doubt. Do not shrink either the headline or the sub-headline below thumbnail-readable size.

MOOD & STYLE
Bright, clean, energetic direct-response with a winter-heating tilt. Lean into a cool-toned palette — pick a fresh combination per generation from the bright-cyan / sky-blue / icy-white / deep-navy family, with one strong contrasting accent (warm gold, hot orange, or bright red) reserved for the price numeral and discount badge so the offer pops against the cool background. Subtle scattered snowflake / frost-particle accents are appropriate seasonal dressing — keep them light decorative texture, never overwhelming. Pick fresh font pairings per generation: bold heavy display sans-serif for the headline and price numeral (confident, slightly aggressive, retail-loud); clean modern sans-serif for body and value stack. NOT moody, NOT dark, NOT premium-restrained, NOT corporate-flat. This is a scroll-stopping winter sale.

LAYOUT (top to bottom)

1. HEADLINE — top third, full canvas width, dominant element
Stacked across two lines, oversized bold uppercase, centred:
   - Line 1: "QUEENSLAND" — set in white (or the lightest palette tone) on the cool background. Heavy display weight, tracked tight.
   - Line 2: "DUCTED HEATING SALE" — mixed weights for rhythm, mirroring the reference: "DUCTED HEATING" set heaviest in deep navy or near-black for hard contrast, "SALE" set in white or the warm accent colour as the closing visual punch.
The headline fills ~25–30% of canvas height. Tight leading, generous side breathing room. This is the loudest element on canvas.

2. SUB-HEADLINE — directly under the headline, centred, full readable size
Medium-weight regular sentence case, clearly readable at thumbnail (NOT fine print):
"Whole-home ducted heating & cooling, professionally installed across QLD."
The phrase "DUCTED HEATING & COOLING" must read unmissably — this line is the clarity guarantee.

3. HERO CONDENSER — right side, mid canvas, ~35–40% canvas width
Place IMAGE 2 here AS PROVIDED. Soft circular glow or subtle radial halo behind it in a slightly lighter palette tone for separation from the cool background. The unit's structure, fans, vents, branding, and proportions remain identical to the source. Vertical centre of the condenser sits roughly on the mid-canvas line.

4. PRICE BLOCK — left side, mid canvas, sized to compete with the headline for second-loudest attention
Stacked left-aligned text on a clean area of background:
   - Small uppercase caps in white or navy: "FROM ONLY"
   - Oversized bold dominant numeral in the warm accent colour (gold / hot orange / bright red — pick fresh per generation): "$78"
   - Medium accent uppercase, same accent colour: "/WEEK"
   - Beneath, smaller uppercase caps: "FULLY INSTALLED · $0 UPFRONT"
The "$78" numeral should be the second-largest piece of type on the canvas after the headline.

5. VALUE STACK — beneath the price block, left side, generous spacing
Three tick-bullet items, evenly spaced, clean readable type, ticks in the warm accent colour:
   ✓ Save Big With Government Rebates
   ✓ Locally Installed By QLD Experts
   ✓ Interest-Free Finance Available
(Do NOT write "0% interest" — write "Interest-Free Finance Available".)

6. CTA BUTTON — bottom-left, beneath the value stack
Rounded pill button, high-contrast warm accent fill with white bold uppercase text. The CTA reads exactly: "QUEENSLAND HOMEOWNERS — CLAIM TODAY". This is a long string — wrap across two lines inside the pill if needed ("QUEENSLAND HOMEOWNERS" line 1 / "CLAIM TODAY" line 2) or use a wide pill that spans most of the left column. Sized to read clearly at thumbnail — meaningful primary CTA, not a footer note.

7. DISCOUNT BADGE — top-right corner area, sitting above or just beside the condenser
Circular burst badge, slightly tilted (5–10 degrees), bold high-contrast warm accent fill with white text:
"25% OFF" — "25%" oversized dominant, "OFF" smaller beneath.
Sized large enough to read at thumbnail (~12–15% of canvas width).

8. WARRANTY BADGE — secondary trust element, mid-right beneath the discount badge or beside the condenser
Smaller circular or shield-shaped badge in a complementary cool palette tone with white text:
"5-YEAR WARRANTY" — two-line treatment ("5-YEAR" top, "WARRANTY" beneath).
Visibly secondary to the discount badge but still clearly readable.

9. AUSTRALIAN OWNED TRUST BADGE — small accent, lower-right area
Per HR19 (client.australian_owned === true), generate a small circular seal-style badge reading "AUSTRALIAN OWNED" — match the visual language of the trust badge style guide (navy fill, gold border, cream text, subtle iconography like a stylised map or star). May recolour subtly to integrate with the cool palette while preserving the seal/medal feel. Roughly 8–10% of canvas width — secondary to the discount and warranty badges. Do NOT include a "Family Owned" badge — client.family_owned is false.

10. BRAND SIDE STACK — right edge, vertically stacked beneath or beside the condenser
Three brand logos stacked vertically, evenly spaced, rendered in their native brand colours on a clean white pill or transparent patch over the lighter palette area:
   - ActronAir
   - Hisense
   - Panasonic
Use the real brand logos. This is a vertical SIDE STACK on the right edge, NOT a horizontal footer strip — A7's bottom-strip-as-hero treatment is explicitly forbidden in A8.

11. LOCATION TRUST PILL — bottom edge, centred or bottom-left under the CTA
Small horizontal pill with a location-pin icon and medium-weight uppercase text:
"LOCALLY INSTALLED ACROSS QLD"
Clearly readable, secondary to the CTA but not buried.

GLOBAL HARD RULES
- 1080×1080 square aspect ratio, nothing else
- Maintain ~60px safe-zone padding inside all four edges — no headlines, badges, CTAs, brand stack, or critical text touching or within this margin
- All text readable at thumbnail size on mobile — every label, value-stack item, badge, and pill must be clearly legible at scroll size
- Condenser (IMAGE 2) used AS PROVIDED — no fan changes, no proportion changes, no re-rendering of the unit itself; soft backdrop glow only
- Maximum 3 brand logos — exactly the three listed (ActronAir, Hisense, Panasonic). No Daikin, no Mitsubishi.
- Do NOT include the phrase "0% interest" or "0% INTEREST" or the misspelling "INTREST" anywhere — "Interest-Free Finance Available" and "$0 UPFRONT" are the only acceptable affordability phrases
- Do NOT add people, faces, or hands of any kind — no AI-generated humans (HR04)
- Do NOT add review badges, star ratings, or Google G logos — A10 is the trust/reviews archetype
- Do NOT include any house diagrams or technical illustrations — none are attached as locked components for this ad, and per HR15 the AI must never generate diagrams from scratch
- Do NOT include a "Family Owned" badge — client is not family-owned
- Do NOT include any stock-agency watermarks (HR18) — verify nothing is reintroduced via the condenser embed
- Discount % MUST equal 25% — must match the client's current promo
- Australian English throughout; "$" for AUD
- The headline "QUEENSLAND DUCTED HEATING SALE" must appear exactly as written (case-stylised but wording intact)
- The phrase "DUCTED HEATING & COOLING" must appear unmissably in the sub-headline — this is the clarity guarantee
- Brand stack sits as a vertical SIDE STACK on the right edge, NOT a horizontal footer strip
- Price block ($78/WEEK) must visually compete with the headline for attention — second-loudest element after the headline
- Pick fresh palette and font pairings per generation within the bright-cool / winter-coded family — do not lock into a single look
- If the output reads moody, dark, premium-restrained, or generic-corporate, it has failed — this should feel bright, energetic, retail-confident, winter-coded

headline: BOXING DAY DUCTED A/C SALE
prompt_text:
You are being given five attached images:

IMAGE 1 (reference ad — A9 Black Friday): Use this as a strong STRUCTURAL template. Mirror its split-canvas composition (visual hero on the left half, type-led offer panel on the right half), its oversized stacked holiday-name type treatment with a coloured outline-box framing the holiday wordmark, its "DISCOUNT UP TO XX%" massive-numeral block, the "DUCTED A/C" clarity line beneath the discount, the pill CTA button below the clarity line, and the bottom brand-logo strip. DO NOT copy its red/black palette, the Black Friday wordmark, the man pointing, the tablet/phone/zoning visual, or its specific brand logos — Boxing Day in Australia is a summer-tropical holiday and needs a different palette and a no-people hero.

IMAGE 2 (locked component cond_neutral_1 — double-fan neutral condenser): Use this image AS-IS as the hero on the left half. Do not modify the unit's shape, fan count, vents, casing, or proportions. You may apply a very subtle tint to the BACKDROP behind the unit (NOT the unit itself) to integrate it with the ad's summer-tropical mood. The unit's structure must remain identical to the source.

IMAGE 3 (cond_neutral_2), IMAGE 4 (cond_daikin), IMAGE 5 (cond_mitsubishi): Reference only — do NOT place these in the output. cond_neutral_1 (IMAGE 2) is the chosen hero, and the client's brand stack is ActronAir / Hisense / Panasonic — Daikin and Mitsubishi-branded units would mislead viewers and must be excluded.

Create a square 1080×1080 social media ad for Jonathan Air, a residential ducted air conditioning installer servicing Queensland, Australia.

FUNNEL CONTEXT
This ad is for a HOT audience — buyers who already know what ducted A/C is and are deciding when to pull the trigger. The job is calendar-bound urgency: Boxing Day is the deadline, the holiday IS the reason to act NOW. The holiday wordmark must dominate the visual story.

CRITICAL CLARITY RULE
A scroller must understand within ONE SECOND that this is a BOXING DAY SALE on DUCTED AIR CONDITIONING. The holiday name dominates first; "DUCTED A/C" appears unmissable as the second-loudest typographic element. Festive aesthetic does not override this — clarity beats cleverness. If "DUCTED A/C" reads as a footer note rather than a headline-tier element, the ad has failed.

MOOD & STYLE — AUSTRALIAN BOXING DAY = SUMMER-TROPICAL
Pick a fresh summer-tropical palette per generation that codes the Aussie post-Christmas beach holiday. Working family options: bright sun-yellow + ocean turquoise/aqua + crisp white + deep navy accent for type weight; OR hot coral/sunset orange + turquoise + white + deep navy; OR turquoise-dominant + sandy cream + sun-yellow accents + dark navy. Avoid the muted retail-red/black of Black Friday — this should feel sun-drenched, optimistic, holiday-mode, but still loud enough to drive a sale. Avoid Christmas red/green entirely.

Type pairing: pick one strong bold display sans-serif for the holiday wordmark and ONE contrasting elegant script (or italic serif) accent typeface for the supporting word "Mega Sale" — mirrors the reference's bold-sans + script combination. Pick the specific fonts fresh per generation.

Festive accent: add subtle diagonal sun-ray streaks emanating from the upper-left corner across the left-half backdrop only. Soft, low-opacity, supports the mood without competing with the condenser. No palm leaves, no beach scenes, no cartoon icons — keep it graphic and refined.

LAYOUT (split canvas — left half visual hero, right half type-led)

LEFT HALF — visual hero on a coloured tropical-summer backdrop

1. BACKDROP
The left half is a flat or subtly gradient coloured panel in the dominant tropical-summer accent (turquoise, hot coral, or sunset orange — pick one fresh). Add the soft diagonal sun-ray streak overlay from the upper-left corner — low opacity, supports the mood, doesn't compete.

2. CONDENSER HERO — centred on the left half
Place the attached cond_neutral_1 (IMAGE 2) centred on the left-half backdrop, sized to occupy roughly 60% of the left-half height. Photo-real, AS-IS. Add a soft drop-shadow grounding it on the panel. The unit reads as the product being discounted.

3. SUPPORTING ACCENT — small circular sun-burst sticker
A small circular sun-burst graphic in a bright sun-yellow or white floating in the upper-right of the left-half panel (above the condenser, not covering it), reading "SUMMER SAVINGS" in tight uppercase. Roughly 15% of the left-half width. Adds festive energy and palette coherence; secondary to the condenser.

RIGHT HALF — type-led offer panel on a clean white or pale-cream background

4. HOLIDAY WORDMARK — top of the right half, dominant element of the entire ad
Stacked across two lines, framed inside a thin outline-box (precisely aligned, not tilted) drawn in the dominant tropical accent colour from the left-half backdrop:
   - "BOXING" oversized bold uppercase, deep navy
   - "DAY" oversized bold uppercase same weight, on the second line, deep navy
   - "Mega Sale" in a contrasting elegant script accent typeface, sized smaller, sitting just beneath "DAY" and overlapping the bottom edge of the outline-box for the same visual signature as the reference's "Super Sale" treatment. Render "Mega Sale" in the hot accent colour (coral / orange / red) so it pops against the white panel.

5. DISCOUNT BLOCK — middle of the right half, centred
Stacked, centre-aligned:
   - Small uppercase caps in deep navy: "DISCOUNT UP TO"
   - Massive bold dominant numeral: "50%" — the largest single element on the right half after the holiday wordmark, rendered in the hot accent colour with a subtle soft glow. The "%" symbol slightly smaller than the "50" but still oversized.

6. PRODUCT CLARITY LINE — directly beneath the discount block
"DUCTED A/C" in bold uppercase deep navy, sized at headline-tier weight (clearly readable at thumbnail, NOT a footer note). This is the HR13 clarity anchor and must read as the second-loudest element on the right half after the holiday wordmark.

7. CTA BUTTON — beneath the clarity line, centred
Rounded pill button in the hot accent colour with white bold uppercase text: "CLAIM TODAY". High-contrast against the white background. Sized to be the obvious action element.

8. VALUE STACK — three short tick-bullets, beneath the CTA
Comfortably readable at thumbnail size, dark navy text, accent-colour ticks:
   ✓ Holiday Bonuses Worth $1,299
   ✓ Locally Installed
   ✓ $0 Upfront

9. AUSTRALIAN OWNED TRUST SEAL — small accent, lower-right corner of the right half
Generate a fresh circular trust seal in the navy / cream / gold visual language: navy circular field, cream/gold border ring, "AUSTRALIAN OWNED" wrapping the top arc, with a simple stylised gold Australian map silhouette or Southern Cross icon centred. Roughly 12-14% of canvas height — meaningful but secondary, not tiny, not dominant. Do not generate a Family Owned badge — the client is not family-owned.

BOTTOM STRIP — full canvas width

10. BRAND LOGOS
Thin horizontal strip across the very bottom edge with three brand logos evenly spaced and rendered in their NATIVE BRAND COLOURS: ActronAir, Hisense, Panasonic. Use the real brand logos. Sit the strip on a clean white or pale band so the logos read clearly. Do not substitute Daikin or Mitsubishi.

GLOBAL HARD RULES
- 1080×1080 aspect ratio (square), nothing else
- Maintain 60px safe-zone padding inside all four edges — no headlines, badges, CTAs, or critical text within this margin. The brand strip may sit close to but not touching the bottom edge.
- All text readable at thumbnail size on mobile — every label, value-stack item, and badge legible
- The condenser (IMAGE 2) must be used AS PROVIDED — do not modify the fan count, the unit's shape, vents, casing, branding, or proportions. Subtle backdrop palette/tint adjustment only, never on the unit itself.
- Do NOT place IMAGES 3, 4, or 5 in the output — they are reference-only for this run
- Maximum 3 brand logos (the three named: ActronAir, Hisense, Panasonic)
- Do NOT include "0% interest" or "interest-free" anywhere — "$0 upfront" is the only acceptable affordability phrase
- Do NOT include any people, faces, hands, body parts, or AI-generated humans — A9 is no-people only (HR04)
- Do NOT generate or include any house diagrams, floor plans, technical illustrations, or zoning graphics — none are attached, so omit entirely
- Do NOT include any stock-agency watermarks (Shutterstock, Adobe Stock, iStock, etc.) anywhere in the output (HR18)
- Do NOT include a Family Owned badge — client is not family-owned (HR19)
- Australian English throughout — "colour" not "color", "$" for AUD
- The holiday name "BOXING DAY" must dominate the visual hierarchy as the loudest element — it is the entire reason for the ad
- "DUCTED A/C" must appear unmissable as a clear product-clarity headline element, sized at headline-tier weight, not a footer note
- Palette must read as Australian summer-tropical (turquoise / hot coral / sun-yellow / white / deep navy) — NOT muted retail red/black, NOT Christmas red/green, NOT wintery
- If the output reads as wintery, generic-retail, muted, or moody, it has failed — Boxing Day in Australia is bright, sun-drenched, holiday-mode

headline: DUCTED A/C SALE — LIMITED STOCK
prompt_text:
You are being given five attached images:

IMAGE 1 (reference ad): Use this as the PRIMARY structural template. Mirror its overall composition — sky-blue gradient sky in the top third, oversized stacked headline in two blues with one word ("SALE") set in white inside a solid royal-blue block, white horizontal pill strip holding three brand logos sitting tight beneath the headline (overlapping the headline-to-fence transition), a clean white vertical-plank picket-fence wall as the middle/lower backdrop, a bright green grass strip across the bottom ~12%, a navy spec pill on the left mid-canvas, an oversized per-week "FROM $X PER WEEK" price block in royal blue on the left, a photo-real condenser standing on the grass in the lower-right at ~50% canvas height, a thin white footer strip with three royal-blue circular trust pills (wrench / shield / star) evenly spaced edge-to-edge, and a tiny "*T&Cs apply" note in the bottom-right. Match its energy, density, and bright outdoor optimism. DO NOT copy its specific brand logos, price, or exact palette — generate fresh per the brief below.

IMAGE 2 (cond_neutral_1, locked component): This is the ONE condenser to use as the hero. Place it standing on the grass strip in front of the white fence, in the lower-right of the canvas at roughly 45-50% of canvas height. Use the unit AS PROVIDED — same silhouette, same two-fan front-facing configuration, same proportions, same neutral white finish. You may add a soft natural cast shadow on the grass beneath it for grounding, and you may relight subtly to match the bright daylight scene, but DO NOT modify the unit's silhouette, fans, vents, panel detailing, or absence of brand markings. Crop, mask, or paint out any visible stock-agency watermark.

IMAGES 3, 4, 5 (other condenser variants): Provided for reference only. DO NOT use them in this ad — only IMAGE 2 is the hero.

Create a square 1080×1080 social media ad for Jonathan Air, a residential ducted air conditioning installer in Australia.

FUNNEL CONTEXT
Warm/Hot broad-reach sale ad. The audience already knows what ducted A/C is — the job is to convert intent with brand trust + a clean per-week price. CRITICAL CLARITY RULE
A scroller must understand within ONE SECOND that this ad is for DUCTED AIR CONDITIONING. The phrase "DUCTED A/C" must be the unmissable dominant element of the canvas — sized as the visual hero of the entire ad. This rule overrides any aesthetic preference.

PALETTE & MOOD

LAYOUT (top to bottom)

1. SKY GRADIENT TOP THIRD
A soft sky-blue gradient occupies the top third of the canvas, with one or two very subtle cloud wisps and a soft light flare in the upper-left corner. This is the "headline sky."

   - Line 2: "A/C" oversized bold uppercase set in the brighter mid-blue (royal / electric blue), sitting flush-left, with the word "SALE" set immediately to its right inside a solid rectangular block of the deeper blue with white uppercase text inside — visual signature, mirrors the reference's "SALE" block treatment exactly.

Inside, three brand logos evenly spaced with thin vertical divider lines between them, rendered in their native official brand colours and lockups: ActronAir · Hisense · Panasonic.

Exactly three logos — no more, no less. Real official brand marks at correct proportions, NOT redrawn or re-coloured. This strip is the archetype's structural identity — without it the ad fails. Position tight under the headline, NOT floating mid-canvas and NOT down at the footer.

5. WHITE PICKET-FENCE BACKDROP — middle/lower portion
Beneath the brand strip, the background is a clean white vertical-plank picket-fence wall running edge to edge. A bright green grass strip occupies the bottom ~12% of the canvas in front of the fence. Bright, clean, suburban-optimistic. Lighting comes from the upper-left to match the sky.

6. SUB-HEADLINE SPEC PILL — left side, mid-canvas

Inside, small uppercase white text reading exactly: "FULL-HOME DUCTED COMFORT — INSTALLED". Padding inside the pill is generous so the text reads cleanly at thumbnail size.

7. PRICE BLOCK — left side, beneath the spec pill, dominant lower-left zone
Stacked left-aligned text on the white fence backdrop:

   - Line 3: medium-weight uppercase deep-blue: "PER WEEK*"

The "$78" must be the dominant numeral of the price block. Anchors the left side as the offer.

8. CTA BUTTON — beneath the price block, left side
Rounded pill button with white uppercase text on the deeper blue background reading: "CHECK YOUR PRICE". Bold, meaningful size — readable at thumbnail, NOT a tiny footer button. Sized roughly 30-35% of canvas width.

9. CONDENSER HERO — lower-right
Place the cond_neutral_1 image (IMAGE 2) on the grass in front of the fence, lower-right zone of the canvas, ~45-50% of canvas height. The unit stands on the grass with a soft natural cast shadow beneath it for grounding. Use AS PROVIDED — silhouette, fans, vents, panels, and finish unchanged. Subtle relight only to match the bright daylight scene. Crop, mask, or paint out any visible stock-agency watermark on the source image.

10. FOOTER TRUST PILL ROW — along the very bottom edge, MANDATORY STRUCTURAL ELEMENT
A thin white horizontal strip running edge-to-edge across the bottom of the canvas, sitting beneath the grass line. Inside it, three small circular royal-blue badges with white icons + adjacent two-line uppercase text labels in deep blue, evenly spaced left / centre / right:

   - Star icon: "ENERGY EFFICIENT"

Bottom-right corner of this strip, very small grey type: "*T&Cs apply" — small but legible, sitting just inside the safe-zone padding.

GLOBAL HARD RULES

- The condenser (IMAGE 2) must be used AS PROVIDED — no fan changes, no brand-mark additions, no re-rendering of the unit silhouette, no proportion changes. Subtle relight + cast shadow only.

- Exactly THREE brand logos in the strip beneath the headline: ActronAir, Hisense, Panasonic. Real official brand marks, native colours, correct proportions. Not four, not two.
- Per-week price only ($78) — do NOT add a fixed-price installation amount, do NOT add anchor "WAS $X" pricing
- Do NOT add a discount % badge anywhere — A7 explicitly forbids it (that belongs to A6)
- Do NOT add any city banner, "homeowners" header strip, or geographic anchoring — A7 explicitly forbids it (that belongs to A8)

headline: AUSSIE SUMMER GETTING TO YOU?
prompt_text:

Place AS PROVIDED in the lower-right area as a small accent — do not modify shape, ducting, vents, fan unit, proportions, or perspective. A subtle palette/tint adjustment for cohesion is acceptable; structural change is not. The cool blue tone of this diagram IS the "relief" half of the visual story — keep it cool against the hot dominant palette.

IMAGE 4, 5, 6 (other diagram variants — house_3d_warm, house_3d_dark, house_3d_zones): Reference only — DO NOT use any of these. Use IMAGE 3 only.

Create a square 1080×1080 social media ad for a residential ducted air conditioning installer servicing Queensland, Australia.

FUNNEL CONTEXT
Cold/warm summer-season audience. Visceral identification with Aussie summer heat-discomfort, then ducted air conditioning delivered as the relief. Emotional pain hit → product as solution.

CRITICAL CLARITY RULE
A scroller must understand within ONE SECOND that this ad is for DUCTED AIR CONDITIONING as the relief from summer heat. The phrase "DUCTED AIR CONDITIONING" must appear unmissably in the sub-headline directly under the main headline, AND "DUCTED A/C" must appear clearly in the price pill. The visceral aesthetic does not override clarity — both placements are mandatory.

MOOD & STYLE
Hot. Visceral. Uncomfortable, then resolved. Pick a fresh hot summer palette of your own per generation — dominant heat colours (sunset coral, deep orange, hot red, peachy pink, molten amber — choose one specific combination) for the canvas/background, paired with cool relief colours (cool blue, white, ice-pale-blue, navy text) for the price pill and diagram zone. The hot/cool contrast IS the visual story. Pick fresh modern display typography per generation, with one word receiving heavy grunge/distressed/painted treatment as the visceral signature. Slightly aggressive, slightly emotional, scroll-stopping. NOT polished, NOT calm — if the output reads like a real-estate brochure, it has failed.

LAYOUT (top to bottom, left/right zones)

1. TOP DECORATIVE BORDER — full-width, ~6-8% canvas height
A thin band of summer-coded decorative motifs framing the very top edge — sun rays, tropical palm leaves, or heat-shimmer waves rendered in semi-transparent warm tones. The reference ad uses florals; for a summer heat ad substitute hot-season motifs that feel hot, not floral. Must not crowd the headline below.

2. HEADLINE — upper third, full canvas width, beneath the top border

   - Line 1: "AUSSIE" — oversized bold uppercase in clean white or cream, ~12-14% canvas height
   - Line 2: "SUMMER" — oversized dominant, ~18-22% canvas height, set in heavy grunge/distressed/painted typography in the hottest colour of the palette (deep red, burnt orange, or molten coral). Brushstroke texture, torn-edge feel, slightly cracked. THIS IS THE ARCHETYPE'S VISCERAL SIGNATURE — without grunge treatment on this word, the ad has failed.
   - Line 3: "GETTING TO YOU?" — medium-weight uppercase, ~9-10% canvas height, white or cream
The headline should feel like rhythm — different weights and a hot grunge accent — not a uniform block.

3. SUB-HEADLINE — directly beneath the headline, full-width
Italic medium-weight, white or cream, clearly readable at thumbnail size (~4-5% canvas height): "Whole-home ducted air conditioning — installed before the next heatwave hits."
This sub-headline is mandatory — it carries the HR13 product clarity.

4. ILLUSTRATED REACTION CHARACTER — left side, mid canvas, ~35-40% canvas height
A stylised flat-vector illustrated character (NOT photoreal — flat illustration aesthetic matching the reference ad's style) of an Australian adult visibly suffering in summer heat. Wiping forehead with one hand, single sweat droplet, slight grimace, simple t-shirt. A small flat-illustrated cartoon sun icon beside or above their head emitting hot rays. Place left-of-centre, vertically centred in the mid canvas zone. Keep simple and graphic — no photoreal rendering.

5. PRICE PILL — right side, vertically aligned with the reaction character, ~35% canvas width
Horizontal rounded pill panel on a COOL relief-blue background (cool blue or ice-pale-blue) with a subtle white border glow — the "answer" to the heat. Inside, vertically stacked text in dark navy:
   - Top line, small uppercase caps (~3% canvas height): "DUCTED A/C INSTALLED FOR"
   - Middle, oversized bold dominant numeric (~14-16% canvas height): "$78"
   - To the right of the dollar figure, smaller uppercase caps (~3% canvas height): "PER WEEK"
The cool blue pill against the hot canvas is the relief moment — this temperature contrast must be unmissable.

6. VALUE STACK — lower-left, beneath the reaction character
Two stacked tick-bullet items (icon + text), evenly spaced, in white or cream text with a cool-blue circular tick icon, separated by a thin cool-blue divider line. Each row ~5-6% canvas height:
   ✓ $1,299 FREE Bonuses
   ✓ $0 Upfront

7. CTA BUTTON — bottom-centre, beneath the value stack, ~50-55% canvas width × ~7% canvas height
Rounded pill button in cool blue with white bold uppercase text reading exactly: "ESCAPE THE HEAT — GET A QUOTE"
(Note: the season-aligned CTA copy reads HEAT, not COLD — this is the summer variant.) Add a small white chevron arrow ">" at the right inside edge of the button.

8. DISCOUNT BADGE — top-right corner, slightly tilted (5-10°), ~16-18% canvas width
Circular burst badge with scalloped/starburst edge. High-contrast cool-blue background with white bold uppercase text: "25% OFF". Sized large enough to read clearly at thumbnail. Sits over the upper portion of the canvas without overlapping the headline. Note: the bonus value lives in the value stack so this badge carries the discount %.

9. HOUSE DIAGRAM — lower-right zone, ~25-30% canvas height (visibly smaller than the reaction character — never larger)

Subtle palette tint to integrate with the cool relief zone is acceptable. The diagram acts as a tertiary explainer/trust element — secondary to the reaction character and the price pill.

10. AUSTRALIAN OWNED TRUST PILL — small accent, lower edge area, modest size (~10-12% canvas width)
Small circular seal badge generated in the trust-badge visual language: navy/cream/gold palette with a stylised Australia map silhouette or southern-cross icon and "AUSTRALIAN OWNED" uppercase text. Meaningful but secondary — sits along the lower edge area between the value stack and the diagram, or tucked above the bottom decorative border, without competing with the CTA, the diagram, or the discount badge.

Mirror the top decorative motif band (sun rays / tropical leaves / heat shimmer) along the bottom edge above the brand strip, semi-transparent warm tones.

12. BRAND STRIP — very bottom edge, full-width, ~8% canvas height
Thin horizontal strip on a contrasting light/cream band at the very bottom edge with three brand logos evenly spaced and rendered in their native brand colours: ActronAir, Hisense, Panasonic. Use the real brand logos.

GLOBAL HARD RULES

- Maintain 60px safe-zone padding inside all four edges — no headlines, badges, CTAs, or critical text within this margin. Brand strip may sit close to but not touching the bottom edge.

- Do NOT include "0% interest" — finance language is not used in this ad

- Do NOT add a fixed-price installation amount or anchor strikethrough — per-week price ONLY (A5 archetype rule)
- Do NOT add additional people, faces, or hands beyond the single illustrated reaction character

- "DUCTED AIR CONDITIONING" must appear unmissably in the sub-headline AND "DUCTED A/C" must appear clearly in the price pill — both placements mandatory (HR13)
- "SUMMER" must receive heavy grunge/distressed/painted typographic treatment in the hottest palette colour — the archetype's defining visual signature
- The hot canvas / cool price-pill-and-diagram contrast is the visual story — if both halves end up the same temperature, the ad has failed

- Family-owned trust badge is OMITTED (client.family_owned is false). Australian-owned badge is INCLUDED (client.australian_owned is true) per HR19

Create a square 1080×1080 social media ad for Jonathan Air, a residential ducted air conditioning installer servicing Queensland, Australia.

FUNNEL CONTEXT

No explainer work needed — the viewer already knows what ducted A/C is.

CRITICAL CLARITY RULE
A scroller must understand within ONE SECOND that this ad sells DUCTED AIR CONDITIONING as the answer to overpaying for power. "DUCTED A/C" must appear unmissably in the solution panel — sized as the dominant product callout, on the second-largest type tier of the whole ad (only the discounted price is larger). Premium aesthetic does not override this rule. Clarity beats cleverness.

MOOD & STYLE
Bold, dark, urgent, problem-led. Pick a fresh dark high-contrast palette per generation — deep charcoal/near-black background paired with one strong saturated accent colour for the solution panel, the key-noun headline word, and the discounted price (think rich crimson, deep amber, hot orange, burnt copper, or oxblood — pick something with weight, not bargain-bin red, and don't default to the gold standard's yellow or the reference ad's purple). Pick aggressive bold display typography for the headline with a slightly weathered/distressed grunge texture on the dominant words for visceral edge — pick a fresh font pairing per generation, do not template. All text must be unmissable at thumbnail size. Direct-response, slightly aggressive, scroll-stopping — but professional, not cheap.

LAYOUT (top to bottom, left/right)

1. HEADLINE — top third, left-aligned, runs wide across upper two-thirds of canvas
Two-part split, stacked across two or three lines:
   - "STOP" set in medium-weight uppercase, off-white or pale cream, sized roughly 50% of the dominant line height — sets the pain command
   - "OVERPAYING FOR POWER" set in oversized bold uppercase in the saturated accent colour — the key noun, dominant element of the headline tier, slightly weathered/distressed type texture for visceral edge
No question mark — this is a command, not a question. The headline must breathe across the upper-left two-thirds of the canvas, not crammed into a corner.

2. HERO VISUAL — mid-canvas left, roughly 45% canvas width × 45% canvas height
A photo-real close-up of a stack of unpaid Australian-style power bills on a dark surface. The top bill is prominently stamped with a red "OVERDUE" or "FINAL NOTICE" mark. Show large dollar figures on the visible bill but no specific identifying details — no real customer names, no real account numbers, no real provider branding or logos, no real addresses. Dramatic side-lighting from one side, deep shadow on the other. Slightly desaturated, moody, cinematic. No people, no hands, no faces. The bills should sit on the same dark surface as the canvas background so they feel embedded in the scene, not floating cut-outs. Absolutely no stock-agency watermarks (Shutterstock, Adobe Stock, iStock, Getty) anywhere in or behind the bills — if any agency mark would otherwise appear, it must be cropped, masked, or rendered out entirely.

3. SOLUTION PANEL — right side, vertical block, roughly 40% canvas width, full mid-canvas height
A solid saturated accent-colour block (the strongest colour in the palette) sitting flush to the right edge or with a small dark margin. Inside, stacked left-aligned text:
   - Top: a small black pill containing "THE SOLUTION:" in white uppercase caps, sized to read at thumbnail (not a fine-print accent — meaningful tier)
   - Beneath: medium-bold uppercase, white, two lines: "DUCTED A/C, FULLY INSTALLED" — must be unmissable, this is where product clarity lives, sized as the second-largest type tier on the whole ad
   - Beneath, smaller, struck through with a clean diagonal line: "WAS $7,990"
   - Beneath, oversized bold dominant numeral in white or near-white: "$5,990" — the largest number on the canvas, this is the offer

4. CTA BUTTON — bottom-left, beneath the hero visual
Rounded pill button reading "CLAIM THIS OFFER" in bold uppercase. Roughly 35–40% of canvas width, sized as a meaningful primary action — NOT a footer element. High-contrast: accent-colour fill with white text, OR white/cream fill with near-black text, whichever reads loudest against the dark background. Small right-pointing arrow icon at the right end of the pill.

5. URGENCY LINE — small italic text directly beneath the CTA pill
"Hurry — 5-Day Install Guarantee" in italic medium-weight, off-white or cream. Readable at thumbnail.

6. BADGE — bottom-right corner, circular burst, slightly tilted (5–10 degrees)
Burst-shaped circular badge in a contrasting accent (gold, bright amber, or a second saturated tone — needs to pop against both the dark background AND the solution panel — gold/cream typically reads strongest here). Roughly 22–25% of canvas width — meaningful, unmissable, the archetype's defining urgency device. Two-tier text inside, tightly composed:
   - Top arc / upper line: "ONCE-A-YEAR PRICING" curved or straight uppercase, smaller secondary tier
   - Centre / dominant: "25% OFF" oversized bold uppercase — the discount value is the loudest element of the badge
This satisfies the discount-percent visibility requirement while preserving the operator-selected urgency phrase.

7. BRAND STRIP — very bottom edge, full canvas width
Thin horizontal strip on a slightly lighter or contrasting band (deep grey or near-black with subtle separation from the main canvas) containing three brand logos evenly spaced, rendered in their native brand colours: ActronAir, Hisense, Panasonic. Use the real brand logos. Do not invent stylised versions. Strip sits close to but not touching the bottom edge.

GLOBAL HARD RULES

- The visible discount of "25% OFF" must appear inside the badge as its dominant element (HR09)
- Do NOT include "0% interest" or "interest-free" anywhere
- Do NOT add any people, faces, or hands anywhere in the composition
- Do NOT use AI-generated faces (reinforced — none should appear at all)
- Do NOT add review badges, star ratings, or trust seals — this is a direct-response sale ad, not a trust ad. The "Australian Owned" trust badge is omitted by design for this archetype's composition integrity.
- Do NOT generate any house diagram, ducting cutaway, or technical illustration of any kind — bills hero only. If diagrams aren't supplied as locked components, they don't appear.
- Do NOT include any visible stock-photo watermarks, agency overlays, or watermark text anywhere in the composition. The bills hero in particular must be rendered clean.
- Pick a fresh palette and font pairing per generation — do not default to the gold standard's yellow/red, the reference ad's purple, or any single recurring "A4 look". The output should feel like a fresh take, not a recoloured copy.
- The accent-colour family must unify the design: the solution panel colour, the key-noun headline word, and the strikethrough/price colour should all share one palette family. The badge's contrasting accent (gold) is the only permitted second hue. The ad must read as ONE unified piece, not three competing accent zones.
- If the output feels calm, polished, or premium, it has failed — this should feel urgent, slightly aggressive, scroll-stopping.
