prompt_text:
You are being given six attached images:

IMAGE 1 (reference ad — structural template): Mirror this ad's composition rhythm and density — left-half text-led, right-half house diagram, dark value-pill strip across the lower third, circular discount badge bottom-right, brand logos along the very bottom edge. DO NOT copy its exact headline, palette, anchor-price treatment, or specific colour decisions — generate fresh ones per the brief below. This A1 ad is per-week-price-only, so the reference's struck-through anchor price ($13,000) must NOT appear in the output.

IMAGE 2 (gold-standard output — quality bar): Calibration reference for typography rhythm, brand-strip polish, and the level of finish expected. Do not copy its layout (different sub-variant — bill-holder hero) — match its quality.

IMAGE 3 (locked component — house_3d_blue): This is the hero diagram. Use AS-IS — do not redraw, do not regenerate the ducting, vents, condenser, silhouette, or proportions. You may apply only a very subtle palette tint shift to integrate with the ad's chosen palette; the diagram's structure must remain identical to the source. The cool blue tones of this asset are exactly the right read for a calm, savings-led ad.

IMAGES 4, 5, 6 (alternative locked diagrams — DO NOT USE): Warm/dark/zoned variants supplied for context only. They belong to other archetypes. Use ONLY Image 3 as the hero diagram. Ignore Images 4, 5, and 6 entirely in the final composition.

Create a square 1080×1080 social media ad for Jonathan Air, a residential ducted air conditioning installer in Queensland, Australia.

FUNNEL CONTEXT
This is a Cold/Warm savings-angle ad. The audience is Queensland homeowners feeling the pinch of rising electricity bills who haven't yet committed to a solution. The job is to position ducted A/C as the calm, intelligent way to reduce their bills — not pain, not urgency, just a confident benefit-led promise. The ad should feel trustworthy and authoritative, like advice from a category expert.

CRITICAL CLARITY RULE
A scroller must understand within ONE SECOND that this ad is for DUCTED AIR CONDITIONING and that the promise is LOWER ELECTRICITY BILLS. The phrase "DUCTED A/C" must be unmissable in the headline — sized as a meaningful part of the dominant type, not shrunk into a sub-line. Both the product category AND the savings promise must be readable in one beat at thumbnail size.

MOOD & STYLE
Calm, trustworthy, benefit-led, savings-focused. Pick a fresh modern palette per generation built around clean cool tones that complement the blue house diagram — soft cool greys, off-whites, with one strong accent colour (cool teal, sky blue, fresh green, or deep navy works well) for the savings-promise word and the price block. Avoid aggressive reds, dark moody backgrounds, or bargain-bin colour combinations — this is not a pain or sale ad. Pick clean confident typography per generation — bold sans-serif display for the headline, with one word or phrase set in a contrasting weight or accent colour for rhythm. Generous breathing room. The whole ad should feel like a modern energy-efficiency brand, not a discount retailer.

LAYOUT (mirroring the reference ad's structure — left text / right diagram / lower-third dark value strip)

1. HEADLINE — upper-left, dominant, occupying roughly the top 35% of the canvas's left half
The picked headline reads exactly: "SLASH YOUR ELECTRICITY BILLS WITH DUCTED A/C"
Stack across four lines with rhythm — different weights and sizes for emphasis, NOT a uniform stacked block:
   - Line 1: "SLASH YOUR" — medium-weight uppercase, smaller scale
   - Line 2: "ELECTRICITY BILLS" — oversized bold uppercase, the visual anchor of the headline
   - Line 3: "WITH" — small uppercase caps set inside a small accent-colour pill (echo the reference ad's "BY UP TO" pill treatment)
   - Line 4: "DUCTED A/C" — oversized bold uppercase in the contrasting accent colour, scaled to match Line 2's weight — the product clarity element, must be unmissable

2. PRICE BLOCK — middle-left, beneath the headline, occupying roughly 20% canvas height
Stacked left-aligned:
   - Small uppercase caps: "DUCTED A/C INSTALLED FOR ONLY"
   - Oversized bold dominant numeral: "$78"
   - Medium accent-colour caps to the right of or beneath the numeral: "PER WEEK"
Do NOT include any struck-through anchor price, fixed price, or "WAS $X" — per-week-only ad. Keep the price block clean and singular.

3. HERO DIAGRAM — right half, roughly 50% canvas width, vertically centred between top edge and the dark value strip
Place the attached house_3d_blue diagram (IMAGE 3) as the hero on the right side. Use AS-IS — do not modify the silhouette, ducting paths, vents, condenser, or proportions. The diagram should sit on the clean light background so its blue tones read clearly. Add a single subtle accent-colour angular geometric shape (triangle or wedge) behind the diagram echoing the reference ad's angular accent shapes — this integrates the diagram into the composition without modifying it. The diagram itself stays untouched.

4. VALUE STRIP — full canvas width, lower third, on a dark contrasting band (deep navy or charcoal)
A horizontal dark band running edge-to-edge across the lower ~22% of the canvas. Inside it, four mini value-pill columns separated by thin vertical accent-colour divider lines, evenly spaced left-to-right, each line readable at thumbnail size:
   - Column 1: "Smart Zoning Technology"
   - Column 2: "Heat or Cool Just the Rooms You Use"
   - Column 3: "5-Year Workmanship Guarantee"
   - Column 4: "$0 Upfront"
Each pill is meaningful secondary copy, not a decorative footnote. Use clean sans-serif at a weight that holds up against the dark band.

5. CTA BUTTON — sitting on the dark value strip, anchored to the left side, vertically centred within the band
Rounded pill button reading: "GET A FAST QUOTE"
Bold uppercase, high-contrast accent colour that pops against the dark band. Sized large enough to read at thumbnail — competitive with the value-strip pills, not subordinate to them. Position it so it does not collide with the divider lines; treat the leftmost segment of the strip as a CTA-and-first-pill zone if needed.

6. DISCOUNT BADGE — bottom-right corner, sitting partly on the dark value strip, slightly tilted (5–10 degrees)
Circular burst badge with two-tier text:
   - Outer arc text: "LIMITED TIME OFFER"
   - Centre dominant: "25% OFF"
Bold high-contrast accent colour — the offer hook. Sized clearly readable at thumbnail (~18–22% of canvas width). The percentage MUST read "25% OFF" to match the client's actual current promo — do not render any other figure.

7. AUSTRALIAN OWNED TRUST BADGE — top-right corner area, small accent (~10–12% canvas width)
Small circular seal badge in the navy/cream/gold visual style of the reference trust-badge system: "AUSTRALIAN OWNED" wrapped around a small map-of-Australia or kangaroo icon. Generate fresh in the ad's palette so it sits cleanly. Position it near the top-right corner, tucked above or beside the diagram so it doesn't compete with the bottom-right discount badge. Do NOT include a "Family Owned" badge — the client is not family-owned.

8. BRAND STRIP — very bottom edge, on a clean light or white band beneath the dark value strip
Thin horizontal strip with three brand logos evenly spaced and rendered in their native brand colours: ActronAir, Hisense, Panasonic. Use the real brand logos. Sized to read clearly at thumbnail.

GLOBAL HARD RULES
- 1080×1080 aspect ratio (square), nothing else.
- Maintain ~60px safe-zone padding inside all four edges. The brand strip may sit close to but not touching the very bottom edge.
- All text readable at thumbnail size on a phone — every value-strip pill, the CTA, the price, and the trust badge must be legible.
- Use the house_3d_blue diagram (IMAGE 3) AS PROVIDED — do not redraw it, do not regenerate the ducting, do not modify the silhouette or proportions. Subtle palette/tint adjustment for cohesion only.
- DO NOT generate a house diagram from scratch. The supplied locked component is the only diagram permitted.
- DO NOT use the warm, dark, or zoned diagram variants (Images 4, 5, 6) — they belong to other archetypes.
- Maximum 3 brand logos.
- DO NOT include the words "0% interest" or "interest-free" anywhere — "$0 Upfront" is the only acceptable affordability phrase on this ad.
- DO NOT include any fixed-price installation amount, struck-through anchor price, or "WAS $X" treatment — per-week price only. Do not echo the reference ad's $13,000 anchor.
- DO NOT add any people, faces, or hands — this is a no-people calm savings ad.
- DO NOT add review badges, star ratings, or Google trust marks.
- DO NOT use any pain framing, urgent distressed language, or moody visual treatment — savings angle, not pain angle.
- The discount badge MUST read "25% OFF" — must match the client's actual promo, not an inflated figure.
- The phrase "DUCTED A/C" must appear unmissably in the headline.
- The headline must reference SAVING / REDUCING / CUTTING bills (the picked headline "SLASH YOUR ELECTRICITY BILLS" satisfies this).
- Australian English throughout — "$" for AUD.
- If any stock-agency watermark appears on any embedded asset, crop, mask, or paint it out cleanly.
- The overall feel must be calm, trustworthy, modern, savings-led — if the output reads as aggressive, moody, or discount-bin, it has failed.

headline: WINTER SALE — DUCTED A/C
prompt_text:
You are being given four attached images:

IMAGE 1 (reference ad): This is the GOLD STANDARD structural and stylistic template for this ad. Mirror its layout, hierarchy, palette, typography, badge treatment, and overall composition as closely as possible. Only the picked variable content (headline copy, price number, value stack items, brand logos) changes from this reference. Do NOT invent a fresh palette or fresh layout — use the reference's bright cyan/blue background, soft white snowflake watermark pattern, oversized black sans-serif headline stacked top-left, pink/magenta circular icon bullets with thin pink dividers, black scalloped/starburst badge with yellow %-OFF text, hand-drawn black curved arrow flourish near the price, and the jagged black silhouetted bottom strip.

IMAGE 2 (locked component — house_3d_blue diagram): Use this image AS-IS. Place it in the upper-right area of the canvas, sized and positioned to mirror IMAGE 1's house diagram. ONE house only — IMAGE 1 shows ONE house, the output has ONE house. Do NOT redraw, regenerate, recolour, mirror, duplicate, fan, array, or modify the diagram's shape, ducting, vents, roof angles, garage, or proportions. Subtle drop shadow for separation from the cyan background is acceptable; nothing else.

IMAGE 3 (locked component — Daikin logo): Use AS-IS in the bottom brand strip. Do not recolour, redraw, or modify.

IMAGE 4 (locked component — Mitsubishi Electric logo): Use AS-IS in the bottom brand strip. Do not recolour, redraw, or modify.

Create a square 1080×1080 social media ad for Sharp Air Conditioning, a residential ducted air conditioning installer servicing Central Coast, Newcastle, and Hunter Valley, NSW Australia.

FUNNEL CONTEXT
Warm/Hot audience. Discount-led winter seasonal sale ad — the job is to push buyers past the line with a clear winter promotion, an unmissable 30% OFF discount badge, and a dominant per-week price. Direct-response feel.

CRITICAL CLARITY RULE
A scroller must understand within ONE SECOND that this ad is for DUCTED AIR CONDITIONING on a winter sale. The phrase "DUCTED A/C" must appear unmissable in the upper-left text block. The "30% OFF" discount badge must also be unmissable. Clarity beats cleverness — premium aesthetic does not override the category callout.

PALETTE — DRIVEN BY IMAGE 1 (do not invent fresh)
- Background: bright cyan / sky-blue field matching IMAGE 1, with a soft white snowflake watermark pattern scattered across the entire canvas at low opacity (~20-30%). This snowflake pattern is a DESIGN element generated fresh — it is NOT a stock-photo watermark. The output must contain ZERO stock-agency watermarks (Shutterstock, Adobe Stock, iStock, Getty) anywhere on the canvas.
- Headline & primary type: solid black, oversized bold sans-serif uppercase
- Price numeral: solid black, massive
- Discount badge: black scalloped/starburst circle with bold yellow/gold "30% OFF" text inside
- Value stack icons: solid pink/magenta filled circles with white iconography inside
- Value stack dividers: thin pink/magenta horizontal lines between rows
- Bottom strip: jagged black silhouette edge with a faint dark pattern texture
- Cool palette only — winter ad. Do NOT introduce green, purple, orange, red, or warm seasonal tones anywhere except the existing red diamond mark inside the Mitsubishi logo (which must be preserved AS-IS).

TYPOGRAPHY — match the reference
- Headline: bold heavy sans-serif (extended/condensed-display family, like the reference), oversized uppercase, tightly stacked across two lines, flush left
- Sub-headline ("DUCTED A/C INSTALLED FOR"): medium-weight black uppercase, smaller, two lines, flush left beneath the headline
- Price: massive bold black sans-serif numeral, "PER WEEK" small caps to its right at baseline
- Value stack labels: clean medium-weight black sans-serif, two-line wrapping per row
- Badge text: bold uppercase, slightly compressed, in yellow/gold

LAYOUT (mirror IMAGE 1, top-to-bottom, left-to-right)

1. BACKGROUND — full canvas
Bright cyan/sky-blue field with soft white snowflake design watermarks scattered across at low opacity. Atmospheric, NOT cluttered.

2. HEADLINE — upper-left, top third
Stacked oversized bold black uppercase across two lines, flush left, tightly leaded:
   - Line 1: "WINTER"
   - Line 2: "SALE"
Dominant element of the upper-left quadrant, sitting inside the 60px safe zone.

3. SUB-HEADLINE — directly beneath the headline
Medium-weight black uppercase, two lines, flush left:
   - "DUCTED A/C"
   - "INSTALLED FOR"
"DUCTED A/C" must read clearly at thumbnail — sized as a meaningful element, not fine print.

4. PRICE BLOCK — left side, mid-canvas, dominant
Massive bold black numeral: "$57"
Immediately to the right at baseline: small caps "PER WEEK" in black.
A small hand-drawn black curved arrow flourish sits to the right of "PER WEEK" pointing across toward the house diagram (matches IMAGE 1's arrow flourish).
NO anchor strikethrough price — anchor is not supplied in the price library for this generation, so do NOT invent one. The reference shows an anchor; the output omits it. This is a deliberate deviation from the reference for data accuracy.

5. VALUE STACK — lower-left quadrant, beneath the price block
Four stacked rows, each containing a solid pink/magenta filled circle icon (~50-60px diameter) on the left with simple white iconography inside, and two-line black sans-serif label text to the right. Thin pink/magenta horizontal divider lines between rows. The four rows, in order:
   ● [snowflake icon]   "Silent, Seamless, and Smart Cooling"
   ● [thermometer icon]  "Zoned Control for Maximum Comfort"
   ● [shield/tick icon]  "5-Year Workmanship Guarantee"
   ● [dollar/wallet icon] "$0 Upfront"
Each row sized so the label is clearly readable at thumbnail. Note: the reference shows three rows; the output has four — fit them with slightly tighter row spacing while keeping all text legible. Do NOT shrink the labels into fine print to fit.

6. HOUSE DIAGRAM — upper-right, hero accent
Place the attached IMAGE 2 (house_3d_blue) in the upper-right area, sized and positioned to mirror IMAGE 1's house diagram (~40-45% canvas width, top-right quadrant). ONE house only — match IMAGE 1's count exactly. Use AS PROVIDED — do not modify shape, ducting, vents, roof, garage, or proportions. Subtle drop shadow for separation acceptable.

7. DISCOUNT BADGE — overlapping the upper-left edge of the house diagram (matches IMAGE 1 position)
Black scalloped/starburst circular badge, slight tilt (~5-10 degrees), with bold yellow/gold uppercase text inside on two lines:
   - Line 1: "30%"
   - Line 2: "OFF"
Sized to be unmissable at thumbnail. Without this badge, the archetype loses its identity — it is mandatory.

8. CTA PILL — lower-right zone, where IMAGE 1's reaction model stood
Rounded pill button in solid black with bold white uppercase text: "CLAIM THIS OFFER"
Sized to be clearly readable at thumbnail — meaningful element, not footer fine-print. Sits in the lower-right empty space above the bottom strip.

9. BOTTOM STRIP — full canvas width, very bottom edge
Jagged black silhouette edge (matches IMAGE 1) with a faint dark pattern texture. Sitting on this black strip, centred horizontally, place the two attached brand logos evenly spaced:
   - IMAGE 3 (Daikin) — left side of strip
   - IMAGE 4 (Mitsubishi Electric) — right side of strip
Logos rendered AS PROVIDED on the black strip. The Daikin mark (cyan + black) reads cleanly on black; the Mitsubishi Electric wordmark is black-on-white in source — render the Mitsubishi logo on a small lighter cream/white pill or keep its native white/transparent background so the black wordmark stays legible. Do NOT recolour either logo. Sized to be clearly readable at thumbnail.

REACTION MODEL — OMITTED THIS GENERATION
IMAGE 1 shows a woman in a red top in the lower-right pointing toward the badge. No reaction-photo asset has been attached for this generation, and HR04 forbids AI-generated faces. Therefore, OMIT the reaction model entirely. Do NOT generate a person, face, hand, finger, silhouette, or figure to fill the lower-right zone. The CTA pill plus the bottom strip occupy that space instead.

COUNT-MATCHING (Priority 2.5 — non-negotiable)
Look at IMAGE 1. Count the elements it contains:
- ONE house diagram → output has ONE
- ONE discount badge → output has ONE
- ONE price block with ONE numeral → output has ONE
- ONE arrow flourish → output has ONE
- Value-stack rows: reference shows three; operator brief says four; output has FOUR
- ONE CTA → output has ONE
- Brand logos: TWO (matching the two attached files)
The output must contain EXACTLY these counts. Do NOT add a second house, do NOT mirror or array the diagram, do NOT duplicate the badge, do NOT add a second arrow, do NOT add reaction figures.

GLOBAL HARD RULES
- 1080×1080 square aspect ratio, nothing else
- Maintain ~60px safe-zone padding inside all four edges — no headlines, badges, CTAs, price, or critical text within this margin. The bottom black strip with brand logos may sit close to but not touching the very bottom edge.
- All text readable at thumbnail size on mobile — every value-stack row, the badge, the price, and the CTA must be clearly legible
- Australian English; "$" for AUD
- Maximum 2 brand logos on this ad (Daikin + Mitsubishi Electric only)
- The locked house diagram (IMAGE 2) must be used AS PROVIDED — no redraw, no regeneration, no shape/ducting/vent/roof modification. ONE instance only.
- The locked brand logos (IMAGE 3, IMAGE 4) must be used AS PROVIDED — no recolour, no shape modification, no AI re-rendering.
- Do NOT include the words "0% interest" or "interest free" anywhere — "$0 upfront" is the only acceptable affordability phrase
- Do NOT add any AI-generated people, faces, hands, fingers, or figures
- Do NOT add review badges, star ratings, or trust badges (Family Owned / Australian Owned) — this is a sale-led discount ad and the reference template excludes them; mirror the reference
- Do NOT add an anchor strikethrough price — not supplied in the price library
- Do NOT add stock-agency watermarks (Shutterstock / Adobe Stock / iStock / Getty) — the snowflake pattern is a design element, NOT a stock watermark
- "DUCTED A/C" must remain unmissable in the sub-headline area — clarity beats cleverness
- The 30% OFF discount badge is mandatory and must be unmissable — without it the archetype loses its identity
- The palette must mirror IMAGE 1's bright cyan + black + pink-magenta-icon + yellow-on-black-badge scheme — do NOT introduce foreign colours
- The output must feel like a direct lineal sibling of IMAGE 1 — same layout DNA, same colour DNA, same typographic DNA — with only the headline copy, price, value stack, brand logos, and (deliberately) the omitted anchor and omitted reaction model varying

headline: Get your Ducted A/C in just {{install_guarantee_days}}!
prompt_text:
You are being given six attached images. Read them before composing — they drive every decision below.

IMAGE 1 (reference ad — STRUCTURAL + STYLE TEMPLATE per Priority 2): This is the canonical A2 layout. Mirror it as closely as possible — layout zones, hierarchy, palette, typography, badge treatment, footer. Only the picked variable content and the specific hero photo change between generations. Pull the palette directly from this reference: clean off-white / very pale cool-grey background, faint pale-cyan hexagon pattern accent in the upper-left corner, deep navy headline weight, bright cyan / sky-blue accent for the speed-promise word and the small pill behind "Installed in Just", thin cyan dividers between value-stack rows, warm orange used ONLY on the guarantee badge as a high-contrast scroll-stopper, pure white footer band. Typography: bold modern sans-serif headline (Montserrat / Poppins family) with multiple stacked weights and mixed case; italic medium-weight sans-serif sub-headline; clean icon-led value-stack rows.

IMAGE 2 (gold-standard A2 output — QUALITY CALIBRATION, do NOT copy): Match this calibre. Note especially: "DUCTED A/C" oversized navy uppercase, dominant; "Installed in Just" sits on a small bright cyan rounded pill; "7 Days!" oversized cyan with a slight italic lean; circular orange guarantee badge top-right, slightly tilted, with a white inner stopwatch icon; subtle cyan airflow arcs curving from a ceiling vent down toward the family; three icon-led tick rows with thin cyan dividers between them; brand logo strip across the bottom on a pure white band.

IMAGE 3 (locked component — fam_jumping): Two children, a girl in a denim dress and a boy in a white shirt, mid-jump on a brown couch, holding hands, joyful, in a bright modern living room with plants, soft natural daylight. Use AS-IS — do NOT modify the children, do NOT regenerate faces or bodies, do NOT alter the setting (HR04). The Adobe Stock watermark visible in the lower-left of the source MUST be cropped or masked out of the final ad — zero stock-agency watermarks in the output (HR18). Achieve this by cropping the photo so the watermark area is excluded, NOT by clone-stamping over it. Apply a light cyan-tinted overlay at ~15–20% opacity over the photo for headline legibility and tonal cohesion (per A2 archetype rule), while preserving the bright airy daylight feel.

IMAGES 4 / 5 / 6 (locked brand logos — Daikin / Mitsubishi Electric / Samsung): Use each AS-IS in their native brand colours. Place them across the bottom brand strip, evenly spaced, exactly three logos to match IMAGE 1's count. Do NOT modify the marks, recolour, or distort proportions.

COUNT MATCHING (per IMAGE 1): exactly ONE hero photo, ZERO condensers, ZERO house diagrams, ONE ceiling vent at the top, ONE circular guarantee badge top-right, ONE small cyan "Installed in Just" pill embedded in the headline, THREE value-stack rows, THREE brand logos in the footer strip. Do not multiply, mirror, array, or duplicate any element.

═══════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════

Create a square 1080×1080 social media ad for Sharp Air Conditioning, a residential ducted air conditioning installer servicing the Central Coast, Newcastle, and Hunter Valley region in NSW, Australia.

FUNNEL CONTEXT
A2 Speed Guarantee — install-time promise targeting "I want it now" buyers across cold-to-hot funnel stages. The job is to communicate INSTANTLY: ducted A/C, installed in 7 days, guaranteed. Family-friendly, energetic, bright daylight — the guarantee is the hero idea.

CRITICAL CLARITY RULE
A scroller must understand within ONE SECOND that this ad is for DUCTED AIR CONDITIONING with a 7-day install guarantee. "DUCTED A/C" must be the single largest text element on the canvas. The guarantee badge top-right must be unmissable at thumbnail size.

PALETTE & TYPOGRAPHY (locked from IMAGE 1 — do NOT invent fresh)
- Background: clean off-white / very pale cool grey
- Decorative accent: faint pale-cyan hexagon/honeycomb pattern in the upper-left corner, ~10% opacity
- Headline primary: deep navy (near-black blue)
- Speed-promise accent: bright cyan / sky-blue (on "7 Days!" and on the small pill behind "Installed in Just")
- Sub-headline & body: medium-weight charcoal/navy
- Value-stack icons & dividers: cyan
- Footer band: pure white edge-to-edge
- Guarantee badge: warm orange fill with white inner detail (the only orange in the ad — high-contrast scroll-stopper)
- Headline type: bold modern sans-serif (Montserrat / Poppins family) with mixed weights stacked across multiple lines
- Sub-headline type: italic medium-weight sans-serif
- Value-stack type: clean medium-weight sans-serif

LAYOUT (mirror IMAGE 1 exactly — text occupies the left ~55% of the canvas, hero photo the right ~45%, brand strip across the very bottom)

1. SUBTLE BACKGROUND ACCENT — upper-left
Faint pale-cyan hexagon/honeycomb pattern, ~10% opacity, tucked into the upper-left corner. Pure decoration. Echoes IMAGE 1.

2. CEILING VENT + AIRFLOW VISUALISATION — top-centre extending into upper-right
Across the top of the canvas, render a clean white ceiling air-conditioning outlet/grille (illustrated, photo-real-adjacent — NOT a technical schematic, NOT a cartoon). From the vent, soft cyan airflow arcs sweep down and to the right, curving toward the children below. The arcs are graphic devices — thin curved lines with subtle motion feel — not photoreal additions to the photo.

3. HEADLINE — left half, upper two-thirds, stacked across four lines with mixed weights matching IMAGE 1's rhythm
- Line 1: "Get Your" — medium-weight title case, deep navy, smaller scale
- Line 2: "DUCTED A/C" — oversized bold uppercase, deep navy — DOMINANT element of the entire ad, the largest single text element on the canvas
- Line 3: "Installed in Just" — medium-weight title case in white, set on a small bright cyan rounded-rectangle pill that hugs the text
- Line 4: "7 Days!" — oversized bold sans-serif with a slight italic lean, bright cyan — the speed-promise visual signature

4. SUB-HEADLINE — beneath the headline, left half
Italic medium-weight, navy/charcoal, two lines:
"Enjoy a cool, comfortable home"
"without the wait."

5. VALUE STACK — left half, beneath the sub-headline
Three rows, each with a small cyan circular icon on the left followed by text. Thin cyan horizontal divider lines BETWEEN rows (between row 1 & 2, and between row 2 & 3 — not above row 1 or below row 3), matching IMAGE 1.
- Row 1 — calendar/clock icon: "Installed within 7 days"
- Row 2 — credit-card / dollar icon: "$0 Upfront"
- Row 3 — shield/tick icon: "5-Year Guarantee"

6. CTA BUTTON — beneath the value stack, lower-left
Rounded pill button, bright cyan fill, white bold uppercase text: "BOOK NOW — LIMITED AVAILABILITY"
Sized to be clearly readable at thumbnail — meaningful, not decorative.

7. SMALL DECORATIVE PROP — lower-left corner edge
A small potted plant or woven basket, partially cropped at the canvas edge, adds organic warmth without competing with the text. Echoes the reference's lower-left plant.

8. HERO PHOTO — right ~45% of canvas, full-height
Place the attached fam_jumping photo (IMAGE 3) filling the right side of the canvas, bleeding off the right edge, full-height. Two children mid-jump on a brown couch holding hands, joyful, bright modern living room behind them. Use AS PROVIDED — do not modify the children, do not regenerate faces, do not alter the setting. Apply a light cyan-tinted overlay at ~15–20% opacity for legibility and palette cohesion. The Adobe Stock watermark in the source must be cropped/masked OUT — pick the crop so the watermark area is simply not in the final composition (do NOT clone-stamp across the children).

9. GUARANTEE BADGE — top-right corner, MANDATORY (archetype-defining element)
Circular badge, slightly tilted (~5–10 degrees clockwise), bright warm-orange fill, white inner ring detail. Two-arc text wrapping the badge plus a centre icon:
- Top arc: "FASTEST INSTALL"
- Bottom arc: "GUARANTEED"
- Centre: small white stopwatch / speed icon (clean line illustration)
Sized at ~25–28% canvas height — large enough to be read at thumbnail, dominant in the upper-right zone, clearly competing with the headline for attention. Without this badge the archetype fails.

10. BRAND STRIP — very bottom edge, full canvas width, pure white band
Edge-to-edge white horizontal strip along the bottom. Three brand logos (IMAGE 4, IMAGE 5, IMAGE 6) evenly spaced, native brand colours, vertically centred within the strip:
- Daikin (left third) — cyan/black
- Mitsubishi Electric (centre third) — red/black
- Samsung (right third) — blue
Use AS PROVIDED — do not recolour, do not resize disproportionately, do not alter the marks. Sized for clean thumbnail readability with breathing room above and below.

GLOBAL HARD RULES
- 1080×1080 square aspect ratio — nothing else
- Maintain ~60px safe-zone padding inside all four edges; the brand strip may sit close to but not touching the very bottom edge
- All text readable at thumbnail size on mobile — every value-stack row, the CTA, the badge text, the brand logos
- Use the family photo (IMAGE 3) AS PROVIDED — do not modify the children, do not regenerate faces (HR04)
- The Adobe Stock watermark visible in IMAGE 3 MUST be cropped or masked out — zero watermarks in the final output (HR18). Crop, don't clone-stamp.
- Use the brand logos (IMAGES 4, 5, 6) AS PROVIDED in native brand colours
- Exactly 3 brand logos — matches IMAGE 1's count and HR05
- Exactly 1 hero photo, 1 guarantee badge, 1 ceiling vent, 3 value-stack rows — match IMAGE 1's count
- Do NOT include "0% interest" or any finance language anywhere — the picked value stack does not contain finance copy, keep "$0 Upfront" exactly as picked
- Do NOT include any pricing — this ad is the speed promise, not a price ad
- Do NOT add review badges, star ratings, or Google badges — wrong archetype
- Do NOT add discount % badges — the guarantee badge is the only badge
- Do NOT add any people beyond the two children in the attached photo — no AI-generated faces (HR04)
- Do NOT generate diagrams, condensers, or technical illustrations — none are required (HR17)
- Do NOT add the Family Owned or Australian Owned trust badges — they belong to A10 trust archetypes, not A2 (HR19)
- "DUCTED A/C" must be the single largest text element on the canvas — clarity beats cleverness (HR13)

The blue diagram must be embedded with its silhouette, ducting paths, vents, fan unit, condenser, drop shadow, and proportions IDENTICAL to the source. You may apply only a very subtle tint adjustment for cohesion — do NOT redraw, do NOT modify the ducting, do NOT change the number of vents, do NOT alter the roof structure or the drop shadow.

Create a square 1080×1080 social media ad for Sharp Air Conditioning, a residential ducted air conditioning installer servicing the Central Coast, Newcastle, and Hunter Valley region of NSW, Australia.

FUNNEL CONTEXT
Cold/Warm audience. Save-on-bills angle. The job is to communicate that ducted A/C reduces energy bills — calm, authoritative, benefit-led. NOT a pain ad. NOT an urgency ad. The viewer should feel "this could pay for itself" not "I need to act NOW".

CRITICAL CLARITY RULE
A scroller must understand within ONE SECOND that this is an ad for DUCTED AIR CONDITIONING that SAVES money on energy bills. The phrase "ENERGY BILLS" must be unmissable in the headline. The phrase "DUCTED A/C" must be unmissable in the price block sub-line. Both are non-negotiable. Premium feel does not override this.

PALETTE & MOOD
Calm, trustworthy, savings-led — clean modern home-brand territory. Pick a fresh palette per generation that respects the cool blue tones of IMAGE 3 (the house diagram). A workable palette family: deep navy or charcoal-blue for headline weight, a bright sky-blue or cyan as savings-accent, an off-white or pale grey background, and a single punchy warm accent (lime-green, gold-yellow, or warm coral) reserved exclusively for the discount badge so it pops against the cool palette. Pick fresh exact hues per generation — do not lock to one look. Typography: bold modern sans-serif for the headline (Inter / Montserrat / Archivo weight family), with weight contrast across lines so the headline has rhythmic emphasis rather than reading as a uniform stacked block.

LAYOUT (mirroring the reference ad's structure)

1. HEADLINE — upper-left, dominant, ~38% of canvas height
Stacked across multiple lines with rhythmic weight variation, reading exactly:
   • Line 1: "CUT YOUR" — medium-weight uppercase, smaller, navy
   • Line 2: "ENERGY BILLS" — oversized bold uppercase in deep navy/charcoal — the dominant element of the entire ad
   • Line 3: "BY UP TO" — small, set on a horizontal sky-blue/cyan accent pill with white type, sitting immediately to the left of the percentage
   • Line 4: "30%" — oversized bold numeral in the same sky-blue/cyan, set HUGE — second only to "ENERGY BILLS" in visual weight
Generous breathing room around the headline block.

2. HERO VISUAL — right side, ~55% canvas width, vertically anchored mid-canvas
Embed IMAGE 3 (house_3d_blue) AS-IS, large and dominant. The diagram bleeds slightly off the right canvas edge. Behind it, a soft cyan triangular accent wedge (echoing the reference ad's right-edge wedge) frames the diagram without competing. The diagram is the visual proof of "whole-home ducted system" at a glance.

3. PRICE BLOCK — mid-left, directly beneath the headline, on a clean white panel
Stacked left-aligned text:
   • Small uppercase navy: "DUCTED A/C INSTALLED FOR ONLY"
   • Oversized bold dominant numeral: "$57" (with the "$" smaller and superscripted)
   • Beside or beneath the numeral, smaller stacked: "PER WEEK"
The dollar amount must be unmissable — second visual hit after the headline.

4. CTA BUTTON — left side, between the price block and the trust strip
Rounded pill button in sky-blue/cyan with white bold uppercase type reading exactly: "REQUEST A FREE QUOTE TODAY!"
Confident, clearly readable at thumbnail. Meaningful size — not a footer afterthought.

5. TRUST STRIP — horizontal dark navy band across the lower-middle of the canvas, ~12% canvas height, edge-to-edge
Three short trust pills evenly spaced inside the dark band, separated by thin vertical accent dividers, white type, all readable at thumbnail size:
   • "Silent, Seamless & Smart Cooling"
   • "Zoned Control for Maximum Comfort"
   • "$0 Upfront · 5-Year Workmanship Guarantee"
Meaningful, not decorative-fine-print.

6. DISCOUNT BADGE — bottom-right, circular burst, slightly tilted (~10°), ~16% canvas width
Set in a punchy contrast colour against the cool palette (lime-green, gold-yellow, or warm coral). Two-line text inside, bold uppercase:
   • Top arc: "LIMITED TIME OFFER"
   • Centre: "50% OFF"
The badge partly overlaps the trust strip exactly as in the reference ad.

7. TRUST BADGES (HR19) — small accents along the bottom-left area, just above or sitting in the brand strip band
Two compact pill badges side-by-side or stacked:
   • "FAMILY OWNED & OPERATED"
   • "AUSTRALIAN OWNED"
Navy-outlined cream-fill pills with navy uppercase type, modest accent (small icon or thin border flourish in matching navy/gold). Sized to be readable at thumbnail without competing with the discount badge or price block. Generated fresh in matching navy/cream visual language.

8. LOCATION ANCHOR — small uppercase navy caps, sitting in or just above the brand strip
"CENTRAL COAST · NEWCASTLE · HUNTER VALLEY"
Subtle but readable — anchors the local trust signal.

9. BRAND LOGO STRIP — very bottom edge, ~8% canvas height
Thin horizontal white/cream band edge-to-edge, three brand logos evenly spaced and rendered in their native brand colours: Daikin, Mitsubishi Heavy Industries, Samsung. Use the real brand logos, properly proportioned.

GLOBAL HARD RULES
• 1080×1080 square aspect ratio, nothing else.
• Maintain 60px safe-zone padding inside all four edges. Brand strip may sit close to but not touching the bottom edge.
• All text readable at thumbnail size on mobile — every trust pill, every value item, every label must be legible.
• "ENERGY BILLS" must be unmissable in the headline. "DUCTED A/C" must be unmissable in the price block.
• IMAGE 3 (house_3d_blue) must be used AS PROVIDED — do not redraw, do not regenerate the ducting, do not modify the silhouette, vents, fan unit, condenser, or drop shadow. Subtle tint adjustment for cohesion only.
• Do NOT generate any house diagram, condenser, or technical illustration from scratch — only the locked component is permitted.
• Do NOT include the words "0% interest" or "interest-free" anywhere — "$0 Upfront" is the only acceptable affordability phrase on the creative.
• Do NOT include a fixed-price installation amount — only the $57/week price.
• Do NOT add any AI-generated people, faces, or hands.
• Do NOT add review badges, star ratings, or Google review counts to this ad — those belong to A10.
• Maximum 3 brand logos.
• Australian English throughout — "$" for AUD, "colour" not "color".
• Pick fresh exact hues and font weight pairings per generation — do not lock to one look across renders.
• The mood is CALM SAVINGS — if the output reads as urgent, aggressive, or pain-led, it has failed.
• Final output must contain NO stock-agency watermarks of any kind anywhere in the ad. If any sourced element carries one, crop or paint it out cleanly.

headline: CUT YOUR ENERGY BILLS BY UP TO 30%
prompt_text:
You are being given six attached images for this composition:

IMAGE 1 (reference ad — A1 structural template): This is the MANDATORY structural template for this archetype. Mirror its layout exactly — left-half text block with a stacked headline anchored top-left, an accent-coloured pill embedded mid-headline carrying a connector phrase, a price block beneath the headline, a dark horizontal footer strip running edge-to-edge across the bottom containing trust pills separated by thin vertical dividers, a thin brand-logo strip at the very bottom edge, the hero house diagram occupying the right half and bleeding off the right canvas edge with a coloured geometric accent shape behind it, and a circular tilted starburst discount/guarantee badge sitting in the lower-right corner overlapping the footer strip. Density, hierarchy, and zone geometry are LOCKED to this reference. DO NOT copy its exact palette, exact headline wording, exact price, or exact pill text — those come from the brief below.

IMAGE 2 (gold-standard A1 output — quality bar only): Use this only as calibration for typographic polish, badge confidence, tick-bullet refinement, and overall production finish. DO NOT copy its layout — Image 2 uses a different structure. Image 1 is the structural template; Image 2 is the quality bar.

IMAGE 3 (locked component — house_3d_blue): Use this house diagram AS-IS as the right-half hero. Do NOT redraw, do NOT regenerate the ducting, do NOT modify the silhouette, the airflow arrows, the rooftop condenser unit, the windows, or the proportions. You may apply only a subtle palette tint (5–10% overlay) to integrate it with the chosen palette — the structure must remain identical to the source. HR02 and HR15 forbid generating diagrams from scratch.

IMAGES 4, 5, 6 (locked brand logos — Daikin, Mitsubishi Electric, Samsung): Use these AS-IS in their native brand colours along the bottom brand strip. Do NOT recolour, do NOT redraw, do NOT distort proportions. Place them evenly spaced.

Create a square 1080×1080 social media ad for Sharp Air Conditioning, a residential ducted air conditioning installer servicing the Central Coast, Newcastle and Hunter Valley regions of NSW, Australia.

FUNNEL CONTEXT
This ad targets a cold/warm audience considering ducted air conditioning as a long-term home upgrade. The job is calm, authoritative, savings-led — ducted A/C as the obvious financial decision that pays itself back through reduced energy bills. Not pain framing. Not urgency framing. The mood is confident, modern, benefit-led — the smart-money choice.

CRITICAL CLARITY RULE
A scroller must understand within ONE SECOND that this ad is for DUCTED AIR CONDITIONING with a strong savings benefit. The phrase "DUCTED A/C" (or "DUCTED AIR CONDITIONING") MUST appear prominently in the headline zone AND be reinforced in the price pill — not buried, not shrunk to footnote scale. Clarity beats cleverness. Premium aesthetic does not override this.

PALETTE & MOOD — pick fresh per generation
Calm, trustworthy, savings-led. Pick a fresh modern palette per generation — clean, confident, premium-modern. Suggested mood territory: cool sophisticated tones (deep teal/charcoal/cream, modern navy/sky-blue/white, sage/cream/charcoal, or forest-green/cream/charcoal). NOT aggressive reds. NOT moody darks. NOT the reference ad's exact grey/blue. NOT the gold-standard's exact green. One strong accent colour reserved for the savings numeral, the embedded headline pill, the CTA, and the badge — the same accent colour ties them together. Typography: pair a confident bold sans-serif for the headline with a clean medium-weight sans for the body. Generous breathing room. Should feel like a modern home-brand ad, not a discount-retailer flyer.

LAYOUT — mirrors the reference ad structure (Image 1)

LEFT HALF — text-led on a clean light/cream background

1. TOP REGION HEADER — small, sitting just inside the safe zone, top-left
Tiny medium-weight uppercase caps with dot separators: "CENTRAL COAST · NEWCASTLE · HUNTER VALLEY"
Set in the deep palette colour. Subtle but legible at thumbnail — anchors the local-trust signal without competing with the headline.

2. HEADLINE — upper-left, dominant element of the ad
Stacked headline reading exactly: "CUT YOUR ENERGY BILLS BY UP TO 30%"
Built across four lines with rhythmic weight contrast (mirrors the reference ad):
   - Line 1: "CUT YOUR" — medium-weight uppercase, smaller scale, set in the deep palette colour
   - Line 2: "ENERGY BILLS" — oversized bold uppercase, the visual anchor of the headline, set in the deep palette colour
   - Line 3: "BY UP TO" — small uppercase white text sitting INSIDE a horizontal accent-coloured pill (this pill is the visual signature element, mirroring the reference's blue "BY UP TO" pill)
   - Line 4: "30%" — oversized bold in the contrasting accent colour, dominant numeral, the savings hit
Headline occupies roughly the upper 45% of the left zone with confident scale.

3. SUB-LINE — directly beneath the headline
Medium-weight, clearly readable: "On Your Whole-Home DUCTED A/C"
"DUCTED A/C" set in the deep palette colour and bolded — this reinforces clarity directly under the headline so the product category is unmissable.

4. PRICE BLOCK — beneath the sub-line, left side, mid-canvas
Mirrors the reference ad's price-block treatment:
   - Small medium-weight: "Ducted A/C Installed from"
   - Oversized bold dominant numeral: "$57"
   - Beside the numeral, smaller, stacked: "Per Week"
The price block is a confident statement, not a footnote. Sized to read at thumbnail.

RIGHT HALF — hero visual

5. HOUSE DIAGRAM HERO
Place the attached house_3d_blue diagram (IMAGE 3) as the right-half hero, occupying roughly 50–55% of the canvas width. The diagram bleeds off the right canvas edge for cinematic depth (mirrors the reference). Position vertically centred against the headline. Use AS-IS — same silhouette, ducting, arrows, condenser. Behind the diagram on the right side, place a soft accent-coloured geometric wedge or angled triangle (mirrors the reference's accent-shape treatment) — adds depth and brand-ad polish. Subtle palette tint may be applied to the diagram for cohesion; structure remains identical.

DARK FOOTER STRIP — running full canvas width, ~14–16% of canvas height

6. TRUST PILLS — three short callouts evenly spaced inside the dark strip, each separated by a thin vertical accent-coloured divider line (mirrors reference). Text in white/cream:
   - "Save up to 30% on bills"
   - "$1,299 in FREE Bonuses"
   - "$0 Upfront Cost"
Each pill clearly readable at thumbnail size — meaningful element, NOT decorative footnote.

7. CTA BUTTON — bottom-left, sitting integrated with or just above the footer strip
Rounded pill button reading: "BOOK A FREE ON-SITE QUOTE"
Bold uppercase white text on the accent-colour fill. Confident sizing — pops as the action element.

8. CIRCULAR DISCOUNT/GUARANTEE BADGE — bottom-right corner, overlapping the footer strip, slightly tilted 5–10°
Circular starburst/seal in the accent colour, white or cream text inside, sized roughly 18–22% of canvas width:
   - Top arc: "5-YEAR"
   - Bottom arc: "GUARANTEE"
Mirrors the reference ad's badge placement and treatment.

9. TRUST BADGES — small accent pills, lower-left zone, just above the footer strip
Two small circular seal badges sitting side by side (each ~8–10% canvas width), styled in the deep palette colour with cream/white iconography, generated fresh in the brand's visual style:
   - "FAMILY OWNED & OPERATED" — small house-with-heart icon
   - "AUSTRALIAN OWNED" — small map-of-Australia icon
Per HR19, generate these in a style that matches the ad's palette. Subtle but legible — they sit as supporting trust accents, not competing with the dominant headline or guarantee badge.

VERY BOTTOM EDGE — brand logo strip

10. BRAND LOGOS
Thin horizontal strip across the very bottom on a light/cream band, with three brand logos evenly spaced and rendered in their native colours: Daikin (IMAGE 4), Mitsubishi Electric (IMAGE 5), Samsung (IMAGE 6). Use the locked logos AS PROVIDED — do not recolour, do not redraw, do not resize disproportionately.

PRIORITY HIERARCHY (highest to lowest)
- ARCHETYPE-SPECIFIC RULES dominate: calm savings-led mood, never pain framing, per-week price only (never fixed), house diagram hero AS-IS, max 3 brand logos

GLOBAL HARD RULES

- Do NOT include "0% interest" anywhere — "$0 Upfront Cost" is the only acceptable affordability phrase (HR07)

- Do NOT add any people, faces, or hands — no-people house-diagram variant
- Do NOT add review badges, star ratings, or Google logos to this ad
- Do NOT add urgency language ("limited time", "hurry", "ends soon") — savings-led calm authority, not urgency
- "DUCTED A/C" must appear prominently in BOTH the sub-line beneath the headline AND the price block — unmissable within one second (HR13)
